query: Generate specs from the greeting tool brief.

context.preconditions: A complete BRIEF.md exists for a simple Python CLI tool that greets users by name. The brief specifies Python 3.10+, CLI invocation via 'python -m app hello <name>', exit codes 0 and 1, and clear success criteria.

expected_behavior: Skill locates and reads docs/features/greeting-tool/BRIEF.md, then checks for DECISION.md and BENCHMARK.md in the same directory
Skill extracts at least 1 User Story in 'As a [role], I want [capability] so that [benefit]' format from the brief
Skill runs MoSCoW + WSJF prioritization for each User Story, presenting a WSJF scorecard with Fibonacci scores and asking for confirmation
Skill writes at least 2 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria per User Story covering both happy path and error case with concrete values (exact commands, output strings, exit codes)
Skill writes SPEC.md to docs/features/greeting-tool/SPEC.md following the exact template structure including WON'T Stories section and Implementation Notes

query: Convert the auth brief to specs.

context.preconditions: BRIEF.md exists but has vague sections: Problem Statement says 'users struggle with authentication' without specifics, Solution says 'improve auth' without naming a design principle, and Success Criteria includes 'system works properly' without binary pass/fail criteria. No error formats or exit codes are specified.

expected_behavior: Skill scans the brief for ambiguities before writing and identifies gaps in User Story benefits, error format, and acceptance criteria specifics
Skill asks all clarifying questions in a single message grouped by section, proposing defaults where reasonable
Skill does not proceed to writing Functional Scope or Acceptance Criteria until the user answers the clarifying questions
Skill never invents requirements not present or clearly implied in the brief — stops and asks when a concrete value (error message, HTTP code, output format) is needed

query: Write specifications from the dashboard brief.

context.preconditions: BRIEF.md describes an analytics dashboard with 4 distinct capabilities (data visualization, filtering, export, drill-down). BENCHMARK.md contains key metrics with verified baselines (e.g., '<200ms load time') and spec recommendations including 'Include' and 'Exclude' items. DECISION.md has 3 accepted decisions from prior phases.

expected_behavior: Skill reads BENCHMARK.md and uses verified metrics to ground concrete values in acceptance criteria (e.g., '< 200ms' instead of 'fast')
Skill uses benchmark Spec Recommendations to fill scope gaps and sharpen acceptance criteria boundaries, flagging any conflicts with the brief
Skill identifies 4 distinct User Stories from the 4 capabilities, runs WSJF for each, and assigns MoSCoW labels (e.g., MUST, MUST, SHOULD, COULD)
Skill excludes WON'T stories from Functional Scope and Acceptance Criteria but lists them in the WON'T Stories section with reasons
Skill treats all accepted decisions from DECISION.md as hard constraints and ignores unverified benchmark metrics
